Downloader Enum BLOB Tool (DlEnum)

The Downloader Enum BLOB Tool (DlEnum) builds enumeration data that describes content packages for use with the Content Download Manager.

Usage 

USAGE: dlenum input_desc_file

The 'dlenum' tool takes the specified input file, and produces a number of different output files:

BuildOffer Meta-Data File
content.xbx
Details Files
details.0.bin details.2.bin, etc
Enum BLOBs
enum.0.bin enum.2.bin etc
Run_buildoffer.cmd
A command file to run the BuildOffer tool for this content package. You can run this file directly or cut and paste parts of this file into your own scripts

(In the preceding descriptions, 0 specifies the default language, other numbers indicate Dash language codes.)

Use the BuildOffer tool to turn these files into a content package.

Example Input File 


[package]
OutputFile="\x2\test.xcp"
ContentFolder="C:\xbox\private\sdktools\content\dlenum"

[MetaData]
Premium="No"
TitleID="0xFFFFFFFF"
OfferingID="0xFFFFFFFF00000000"
BitFlags="0xffffffff"


; North America
OfferRegions.0="103,0,0,TaxNotIncluded" ; US
OfferRegions.1="16,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"  ; Canada

; Japan
OfferRegions.2="53,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Japan

; Europe
OfferRegions.3="35,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; England
OfferRegions.4="24,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; France
OfferRegions.5="34,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Germany
OfferRegions.6="90,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Sweden
OfferRegions.7="50,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Italy
OfferRegions.8="8,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"		; Belgium
OfferRegions.9="74,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Netherlands
OfferRegions.10="31,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Spain
OfferRegions.11="25,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Denmark
OfferRegions.12="32,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Finland
OfferRegions.13="5,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Austria
OfferRegions.14="44,0,0,TaxNotIncluded"	; Ireland

ActivationDate="3/1/2002"

AdminFriendlyName="Sample Content Package Friendly Name"


[Display]
;
; Default language
; 
DisplayName="Default Name"
DashDisplayName="Dash Name"
Description="Description"
Image="input\abutton.bmp"
;ImageAlpha=""

[Display.fr]
DisplayName="French Name"
DashDisplayName="French Dash Name"
Description="French Description"
Image="input\bbutton.bmp"

[Display.ja]
DisplayName="Japanese Name"
DashDisplayName="Japanese Dash Name"
Description="Description"
Image="input\bbutton.bmp"

Input File Description

The section describes the format and contents of the dlenum.exe input file.

The input file is a Unicode text file. The file format uses square brackets to specify sections, and lines of the form

Field="Value"

to specify values. Quotes are required around values.

Lines that begin with ';' or a '#' are treated as comments and ignored.

Following are descriptions of the sections and values in the input file.

OutputFile
[Required] Specifies the output xcp file.
ContentFolder
[Required] Specifies the path to the directory that contains the content for the package
Premium
[Required] Specifies whether the offering is for premium content. A value of "Yes" means the content is premium. A value of "No" means the content is free.
TitleID
[Required] Specifies the 32-bit Hexadecimal Title ID for the title with which this content should be used.
OfferingID
[Required] Specifies the 64-bit Hexadecimal Offering ID for this content package. The upper 32 bits should be the same as the TitleID.
BitFlags

[Required] Specifies a bitflag value that identifies the version of the title with which this content should be used. The bitflag allows you to filter content based on region or title version.

For example, if the content should be downloaded only by the Japanese version of the game, then create a Japanese bitflag value such as 0x01000000. Then, when launching downloader.xbe from the Japanese version of the game, pass in this value in the dwBitFilter member of the LD_DOWNLOADER structure.

You can specify additional bitflag values for other versions of the title.

If this content should be available to all versions of the title, specify: 0xffffffff.

OfferRegions.N

[Required] Specifies price information for regions in which the content is offered. Content is also filtered based on the user's billing region. A user (playing any version of the game) can download content only if they in the correct billing region for the content. For example, a US gamer playing an imported Japanese game on an imported Japanese Xbox console could download content only if the content is available in the US billing region.

Most content packages should be available in all billing regions. Filtering should be done using the BitFlags value described previously. To create a content package for each continent, you need to use the -S option on buildoffer.

The format of the region information for each region is as follows: <Country ID>,<price whole>,<price fractional>,<tax type>

For free content, the whole and fractional price must also be zero. Leading and trailing whitespaces are not allowed before and after arguments. Tax type must be set to 'NotApplicable'. If the content is premium, then the whole and fractional prices must be set to the appropriate values. The correct tax type must also be specified. Valid values are: TaxNotIncluded | TaxIncluded | GSTIncluded | VATIncluded | TaxNotApplicable Contact your account manager if you need more information about setting up offering regions.
ActivationDate
[Required] Specifies the data this offer becomes valid. This value determines the order in which content is listed - more recent activation dates are listed first. This value should almost always be a date in the past.
AdminFriendlyName
[Required] Specifies the name used by the Xbox Live operations staff to refer to this content package. This name should be in English.
OfferLocations="1.2.3.4:3074/content/content.xcp"
[Optional] Specifies a download URL for the content package. This URL should be in the form:

"<IP Address>[:<Port>]/<URI of package>"

The extension of the package should be .xcp

This value is for publishers who want to host their own download servers. If this value is not specified, the package defaults to an Xbox-owned content-download server on PartnerNet. Note that all content hosted on PartnerNet may be downloaded and viewed by other partners. You should consider hosting anything confidential on your own servers.

DisplayName, Description

[Required] The DisplayName is one line of text that appears in Downloader's list of content available for download. The Description appears beneath the image that represents this particular piece of content. The Description supports a small set of HTML formatting, as follows:

  • <b> bold text</b> produces bold text.
  • <br> produces a line break.

Example: 

DisplayName="My Online Content"
Description="This description<br>"
"has <b>bold</b> text and<br>"
"line breaks.<br>"

In addition, you may specify alternate display names and descriptions for different languages. If the console that is running Downloader is set to use a language that you have not provided, Downloader displays the default name and description, as described in the preceding example. The following table outlines the keywords you should use for each of the different languages:

Language Display Name Description
English DisplayName_EN Description_EN
Japanese DisplayName_JA Description_JA
German DisplayName_DE Description_DE
French DisplayName_FR Description_FR
Spanish DisplayName_ES Description_ES
Italian DisplayName_IT Description_IT
Korean DisplayName_KO Description_KO
Chinese DisplayName_ZH Description_ZH
Portuguese DisplayName_PT Description_PT

Note    Keep the following in mind when using DlEnum:

  • Downloader does not perform any word-wrapping on the description text, so you need to manually insert line breaks with the <br> tag.
  • The input file must contain Unicode-formatted text. The DlEnum tool does not understand ANSI text files.
  • Because Downloader displays the Description in a proportional font, there is no way to determine ahead of time if Description text will fit in the allotted space. You should preview the text in the Content Download Manager to make sure it fits.

See Also

Finding New Content, Content Download Manager
